I couldn't find any information about the plant Camderia. It is possible that it is a misspelling or a lesser-known plant. If you are referring to Camellia, which is a genus of flowering plants, I can provide the following information:

 Camellia is a plant genus that includes approximately 300 species of flowering plants, commonly known as tea plants or camellias, characterized by their showy, often fragrant flowers and glossy leaves.

The health benefits of Camellia include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce oxidative stress and improve cardiovascular health. The plant's leaves and flowers are also known to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it effective against a range of pathogens.

The bioactive constituents of Camellia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

The most common herbal preparations made with Camellia include tea, which is extracted from the leaves, and infusions, which are made from the flowers or young leaves.
